The Variable

How to Tackle Agentic AI's Memory Problem

Generations of product leads and software developers have lived by the famous project-management triangle principle: "Good, fast, cheap. Choose two." The rise of agentic AI is complicating the picture.

 

These days, it seems like without robust memory infrastructure, you can say goodbye to "good" and "fast." With one in place, "cheap" seems increasingly unrealistic. What's an ambitious AI builder to do?

 

The articles we've chosen for you this week address different facets of the memory problem, introduce practitioners to novel tools and approaches, and offer sharp insights on making AI memory systems powerful — and resource-efficient. 

A Practical Guide to Memory for Autonomous LLM Agents

If you're new(ish) to the conversation around memory and how it plays into agentic workflows, you should immediately head to  Nick Lawson's accessible and comprehensive primer. Based on his realization that what makes agents "actually work isn’t the model choice. It’s the memory architecture," this guide unpacks recent research, defines the different types of memory you should be aware of, and zooms in on the pitfalls to avoid.

Read More

Prefill Is Compute-Bound. Decode Is Memory-Bound. Why Your GPU Shouldn’t Do Both.

Gokul Chandra Purnachandra Reddy devotes his debut TDS article to GPU optimization, and covers the efficiencies that disaggregated LLM inference can introduces into agentic (and other) AI processes. 

Read More

memweave: Zero-Infra AI Agent Memory with Markdown and SQLite — No Vector Database Required

"Models are stateless by design. Each call starts with a blank slate. Memory is your problem to solve." Sachin Sharma proceeds to walk us through the process of building an open-source tool that targets this very problem.

Read More

Modernize Your Data Pipelines for AI

Our latest webinar on pipeline modernization is now available on-demand for teams moving beyond legacy batch loads. In this session, we explore how to evolve your workflows to support the real-time data demands of agentic RAG systems and prevent production failures.

Watch On-Demand

This Week's Most-Read Stories

In case you missed them, here are three articles that resonated with a wide audience in the past week:

  • RAG Isn’t Enough — I Built the Missing Context Layer That Makes LLM Systems Work, by Emmimal P Alexander
    Most AI tutorials stop at retrieval or prompting. But real systems need a layer in between — one that controls memory, compression, re-ranking, and token budgets.

  • How to Maximize Claude Cowork, by Eivind Kjosbakken
    Learn how to get the most out of Claude Cowork.

     

  • 6 Things I Learned Building LLMs From Scratch That No Tutorial Teaches You, by Aman Vasisht
    From rank-stabilized scaling to quantization stability: A statistical and architectural deep dive into the optimizations powering modern Transformers.

Other Recommended Reads

You might be interested in some of the other topics we covered recently — so we hope you explore these excellent reads:

  • The LLM Gamble, by Stephanie Kirmer
    Why it tickles your brain to use an LLM, and what that means for the AI industry.

  • Beyond Prompting: Using Agent Skills in Data Science, by Yu Dong
    How I turned my eight-year weekly visualization habit into a reusable AI workflow.

  • From Pixels to DNA: Why the Future of Compression Is About Every Kind of Data, by Roberto Iacoviello
    How JPEG, MPEG, and AI-native standards are redefining media, machine vision, and even DNA in our data-saturated world.

  • What Does the p-value Even Mean?, by Sara A. Metwalli
    And what does it tell us?

  • .From OpenStreetMap to Power BI: Visualizing Wild Swimming Locations, by Cláudio Tereso
    How to turn OpenStreetMap data into an interactive map of wild swimming spots using Overpass API and Power BI.

From our sister site, The New Stack

From vectors to tensors: Expanding the possibilities of AI search

In this webinar, Vespa.ai experts will dive into how tensors extend what’s possible beyond traditional vector search. They’ll cover the sophisticated scoring needed for complex search problems involving multimodal data and nuanced relevance ranking.

Register Today

From our partner, Cautious Optimism

The SpaceX-Cursor deal is a gutsy Hail Mary

Are their relative strengths truly complementary? Yes. Few companies in history can match xAI’s ability to raise cash, and Cursor has been successfully selling its AI coding product in a market dominated by OpenAI (Codex) and Anthropic (Claude Code).

Read More

Meet Our New Authors

We hope you take the time to explore top-notch work from our newest contributors:

  • Ansh Aggarwal showed how we can leverage deep learning techniques to generate rich Minecraft worlds.

  • Léo Saci explores the feasibility of turning an unsupervised model into a classifier with a minimal amount of labeled data.

  • Benjamin Li shared a lucid and comprehensive introduction to deep evidential regression for uncertainty quantification.

Contribute to TDS

Did your New Year's resolutions include publishing on TDS and joining our Author Payment Program? It's not too late to send along your draft!
Submit Your Article

Want more insights, industry trends, and exclusive content? Follow us on social media for real-time updates and expert discussions!
